title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Safe Footpaths a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on safe footpaths is a fundamental right, grounding it in the constitutional guarantee of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21. The Court directed that all roads must have footpaths to ensure pedestrian safety. The bench also called for legislative action to codify and enforce this right. The ruling has significant implications for urban planning and road infrastructure governance across India.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to the expansive judicial interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life), a recurring theme in GS Paper 2 and Polity. Tests understanding of how the Supreme Court has progressively widened the scope of fundamental rights through judicial activism.
key_facts: Right declared: Right to walk on safe footpaths as a Fundamental Right
Constitutional basis: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Direction issued: All roads must have footpaths
Court also sought legislative framework to enforce pedestrian rights
source: The Indian Express / India Today / The Business Standard

title: India-UK Free Trade Agreement: Absence of Investment Chapter Noted
summary: The recently concluded India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) does not include a dedicated investment chapter, a gap noted by observers including University of Glasgow Vice-Chancellor Anton Muscatelli. Despite this, Muscatelli stated that the absence is not a major concern and that the deal still provides a strong foundation for bilateral economic engagement. The India-UK FTA was signed after over three years of negotiations. It is one of India's most significant trade agreements with a major Western economy.
exam_angle: India-UK FTA is a key current affairs topic for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy). The absence of an investment chapter distinguishes it from comprehensive economic partnerships and is a nuanced point for Mains answers.
key_facts: Agreement: India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA)
Notable gap: No dedicated investment chapter included
Negotiations spanned over three years
Covers goods, services, and bilateral trade facilitation

title: India-US Trade Deal: Piyush Goyal Cites Need for Competitive Advantage; Farmers Fully Protected
summary: Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated that India cannot implement a trade deal with the United States until India has a clear 'competitive advantage' in the areas under negotiation. He emphasized that the interests of Indian farmers are fully protected and will not be compromised in any agreement. The statement reflects India's cautious approach amid US pressure to open agricultural and industrial markets. Negotiations for a Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA) between India and the US are ongoing.
exam_angle: India-US trade negotiations are crucial for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy), particularly regarding agricultural protectionism, trade policy, and India's stance on market access. Piyush Goyal's role as Commerce Minister is relevant for 'Persons in News'.
key_facts: Minister: Piyush Goyal, Union Commerce & Industry Minister
India's position: Trade deal contingent on achieving 'competitive advantage'
Assurance given: Indian farmers fully protected from any trade concessions
Agreement sought: India-US Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA)
source: The Times of India / The Economic Times / NDTV

title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Amid Escalating Israel-Lebanon Conflict
summary: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israel's continued airstrikes on Lebanon, raising global energy security concerns. The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints, through which approximately 20% of global oil trade passes. The move comes amid heightened tensions surrounding an ongoing US-Iran nuclear deal negotiation. Israel's military operations in Lebanon have continued, threatening to widen the regional conflict.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is a frequently asked geographic and strategic chokepoint in UPSC Geography and IR. Iran's action connects to India's energy security (GS Paper 3) since India is a major importer of West Asian oil.
key_facts: Location: Strait of Hormuz — between Iran and the Oman peninsula
Strategic significance: ~20% of global oil trade passes through it
Trigger: Israel's ongoing airstrikes on Lebanon
Concurrent development: US-Iran nuclear deal negotiations underway

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves Google DeepMind for Anthropic
summary: Dr. John Jumper, who won the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his work on AlphaFold — an AI system that predicts protein structures — has resigned from Google DeepMind and joined Anthropic, a leading AI safety company. His move is seen as a significant shift in the AI talent landscape. AlphaFold's breakthrough in protein structure prediction is considered one of the most transformative applications of AI in life sciences. Anthropic, co-founded by former OpenAI researchers, is known for developing the Claude AI model.
exam_angle: John Jumper's Nobel Prize and AlphaFold are important for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech), especially the application of AI in biology/medicine. The governance debate around frontier AI also connects to GS Paper 2 (IR/governance of emerging technologies).
key_facts: Person: Dr. John Jumper — 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry winner
Achievement: AlphaFold — AI-based protein structure prediction system
Previous employer: Google DeepMind
New employer: Anthropic (AI safety company, known for Claude AI)

title: Frontier AI Global Governance Need Highlighted After Anthropic Outage
summary: A recent temporary blackout episode involving Anthropic's AI systems has reignited global debate about the need for international governance frameworks for frontier artificial intelligence. Experts and commentators have argued that the incident demonstrates systemic risks posed by powerful AI systems operating without adequate global oversight. The episode has drawn attention to the gap between rapid AI capability development and the pace of international regulatory cooperation. India, as a major AI consumer and developer, has a stake in shaping such governance frameworks.
exam_angle: Global AI governance is a key emerging topic for GS Paper 2 (IR/International Institutions) and GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech). Connects to India's AI policy, the Global Partnership on AI (GPAI), and G20 AI governance discussions.
key_facts: Incident: Anthropic AI system temporary blackout raised governance concerns
Issue highlighted: Absence of global regulatory framework for frontier AI
India is a member of the Global Partnership on AI (GPAI)
G20 and UN are key forums for AI governance discussions

title: Modi Government's Push to Reverse India's Brain Drain
summary: The Indian government has initiated policy measures aimed at reversing the trend of brain drain — the emigration of highly skilled Indian professionals, particularly in STEM fields, to developed nations. The initiative reportedly involves creating incentive structures including research funding, startup ecosystems, and returnee-friendly policies to attract overseas Indian talent back to India. This connects to the broader National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 goals and the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) framework. India loses a significant share of its top talent annually to the US, UK, Canada, and Australia.
exam_angle: Brain drain and its reversal (brain gain/brain circulation) is a key topic for GS Paper 2 (Social Issues/Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Human Capital). The ANRF and related policy tools are UPSC-relevant specifics.
key_facts: Policy aim: Reverse brain drain by attracting skilled Indian diaspora back to India
Key framework: Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) established under NRF Act 2023
Related policy: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020
India ranks among top countries of origin for skilled emigration globally

title: Karnataka Appoints Two New Additional Advocate Generals in Supreme Court
summary: The Karnataka government has appointed Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals to represent the state in the Supreme Court of India. The Advocate General is the chief law officer of a state, appointed under Article 165 of the Constitution. Additional Advocate Generals assist in handling the state's legal matters at the apex court level. Such appointments are significant for states with high volumes of constitutional and administrative litigation.
exam_angle: The office of Advocate General is constitutionally established under Article 165 and is relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity). Questions on state law officers, their appointment, and role have appeared in Prelims.
key_facts: Appointees: Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals
State: Karnataka
Constitutional provision: Advocate General appointed under Article 165
Role: Chief law officer of a state; Additional AGs assist at Supreme Court level
